overview: The Marathon County Health & Human Services Committee held an educational orientation meeting for new board members, featuring presentations from the Health Department, Veteran Service Office, and Aging Disability Resource Center. No action items were voted on beyond approving meeting minutes; the meeting focused on introducing department missions, services, budgets, and organizational structures to committee members.
committee: Marathon County Health & Human Services Committee

agenda.item: Call to order and remote participation confirmation
Approval of April 1st committee meeting minutes
Operational functions required by statute - no items presented
Review of standing committee ordinances, missions, duties and responsibilities
Health Department presentation by Health Officer Caitlyn Suginaka
Social Services Department overview by Administrator Leonard
Veteran Service Office presentation by VSO Erin Galindo
Aging Disability Resource Center presentation by Executive Director Mike Ray
Next meeting date discussion and adjournment

discussions.body: Supervisor Cavelli made a motion to approve the minutes, seconded by Vice Chair Rosenberg. The motion passed unanimously with all members voting in favor.

Health Officer Caitlyn Suginaka presented the department's mission of preventing disease, promoting health, and protecting from environmental hazards. She highlighted their 37.6 FTE staff, management of over 1,200 licenses, public health vending machines stocked with free resources like tick kits, the Nurse Family Partnership program with first graduates, and a new homeless system response position. Supervisor Kremes asked about pandemic preparedness; Suginaka confirmed they have plans in place, conduct training exercises for measles response, and have a public health emergency preparedness planning body.

Administrator Leonard provided the overview in absence of Director Christa. He described the department's nearly 140 FTE staff, divisions including child welfare, youth justice, children's long-term support, economic support, and child support. He highlighted the successful Family Keys pilot program addressing housing instability, noting that of their $22 million budget, approximately $7 million is tax levy with the rest from intergovernmental revenues and grants.

VSO Erin Galindo reported his 3.0 FTE office had 8,149 interactions in 2025, up from 5,900 the previous year. He noted $44 million annually flows to local veterans, with $2.6 million in retroactive payments in 2025 alone. Galindo discussed initiatives including surviving spouse benefits, housing stability partnerships with Center for Veterans Issues, and suicide prevention efforts - noting 7 of Marathon County's 30 suicides in 2025 were veterans. Supervisor Kremes asked about regional coordination; Galindo explained state statute requires each county to have a VSO but offices collaborate and can serve any veteran statewide.

Executive Director Mike Ray presented on the four-county ADRC serving Marathon, Lincoln, Langlade, and Wood counties with about 60 FTEs and 385-400 volunteers. He described services including information and assistance, caregiver support, dementia care specialists, and the Meals on Wheels program. Ray highlighted significant financial challenges - nutrition program costs increased 40-45% over four years while federal funding remained flat due to non-reauthorization of the Older Americans Act. The Marathon County levy contribution is $413,135.
Vice Chair Rosenberg caught an error in the announced next meeting date. The meeting was corrected from Wednesday May 29th to Tuesday June 3rd at 3 PM, as May 29th is not a Wednesday.

actionItems: April 1st committee meeting minutes approved unanimously
North Central Healthcare to be scheduled for separate full presentation before the committee
ADRC Executive Director Mike Ray to present annual report with 2025 data to full board
Committee members to email Chair Boots and Administrator Leonard with items for future agendas
Chair Boots to bring strategic plan discussion to upcoming meetings
Next meeting scheduled for Tuesday, June 3rd at 3 PM
title: Marathon County Health & Human Services Committee Meeting
